name: Vaccinia_concatemer_resolution
description: Vaccinia replication intermediate: vaccinia DNA replicates through head-to-head and tail-to-tail concatemeric intermediates with junctions located at what becomes the hairpin terminus after resolution. The A22R holliday-junction resolvase cleaves these concatemer junctions to yield mature unit-length genomes with hairpin ends. The concatemer junction sequence (concatemer-junction region, CJR) is a palindromic 200-nt element containing two copies of the hairpin sequence back-to-back. Important for mapping of mid-replication-cycle samples: reads at the CJR appear to span the genome ends and require concatemer-aware interpretation.
expected_copy_count: transient replication intermediate, 0 in mature virion DNA
